name: Indian Oil Marketing (IOC/BPCL/HPCL)
summary: Day 60 Monday — RBI ASKED OMCs TO USE CRISIS-ERA SPECIAL CREDIT FACILITIES [CONFIRMED Reuters via MarketScreener India + ORF]. RBI instructed state-run oil refiners (IOC, HPCL, BPCL — controlling ~50% of India's 5.2M bpd refining) to curb spot dollar buying and use special credit facilities for dollar needs — 'crisis-era measures' active ~2 weeks (since ~Apr 13-15). Second confirmed RBI crisis-era measure of war. STRUCTURAL FUEL-PRICING DETERIORATION CONTINUES: OMCs losing ~₹20/L petrol + ~₹100/L diesel as pump prices frozen ~4 years; India crude basket $100.41 Friday vs February $69.01. PPAC publishing daily international crude rates = preparation tell for fuel-price pass-through. Government forced choice within 30 days: (a) raise pump prices (politically costly, state elections); (b) absorb via fiscal subsidy (~₹2,800 crore/week); (c) restart Iran-rupee oil track via Chabahar despite US pressure; (d) wait for ceasefire/Brent normalisation. BRANCH 3 CONFIRMED MONDAY: Brent gapped -4.6% to $100.47 Sunday Globex → IOC/BPCL/HPCL likely +1.5-3% on diesel margin recovery at cash open. Diesel margin compression unwinds at lower crude. Branch 3 Brent sub-$103 sustained 2-3 sessions = RBI special-credit-facility could be wound down (release rupee-positive flow). IOC Jaya 2M bbl + Reliance Felicity 2M bbl waiver-window completed before April 19 expiry; Russia 30-day waiver extension preserves ~1.9 mbpd Russian flow. Goodreturns/V3Cars Monday fuel prices: petrol ₹94.77/L Delhi, diesel ₹87.67/L Delhi, petrol ₹103.54/L Mumbai.

signals.content: IOC fell 3.1%, BPCL fell 2.8% on March 28 — leading the Nifty decline. Government's duty cut provides relief but OMCs still face margin squeeze with Brent at $112. If government instructs OMCs to absorb losses rather than pass through to consumers (as they did before 2024 elections), these companies become fiscal shock absorbers. Combined market cap erosion estimated at $15-20B since war began. (Sources: yfinance, Business Standard, Economic Times)

Government slashed excise duty: petrol from ₹13 to ₹3/liter, diesel duty eliminated entirely (March 27, effective). This is the most aggressive fuel intervention since 2022. Estimated annual fiscal cost: $15-20B. IOC, BPCL, HPCL marketing margins turned NEGATIVE — selling fuel below cost. Oil marketing companies led market decline: Union Bank -6.55%, Canara Bank -5.21%, Bank of Baroda -5.07% (these are banks exposed to OMC credit, not OMCs themselves). Brent at $105-118 keeps pressure intense. Government faces impossible choice: raise retail prices (politically explosive) or absorb losses (fiscal deficit balloons). STT on futures raised 150% effective April 1 (from 0.02% to 0.05%). (Sources: Al Jazeera, Business Standard, 5paisa)

Oil at $109-112 keeps marketing margins deeply NEGATIVE for IOC, BPCL, HPCL. Government absorbed cost via duty cuts (petrol ₹13→3, diesel eliminated) — $15-20B fiscal cost annually. No further policy space unless government raises retail prices (politically explosive). STT hike effective April 1 adds trading friction. India closed April 3 (Good Friday). RBI MPC April 6-8 may address OMC fiscal burden. (Sources: Business Standard, Al Jazeera)

Indian crude basket at $120.84/bbl — above every analyst forecast scenario. Government already maxed out duty relief: petrol duty slashed from Rs 13 to Rs 3, diesel duty eliminated entirely (March 27). IOC, BPCL, HPCL facing deepening margin squeeze — absorbing the gap between international prices and controlled domestic prices. Annual fiscal cost estimated at $15-20B. No further policy space unless government raises retail prices (politically explosive pre-election). Oil marketing companies are the fiscal shock absorbers of this crisis. RBI MPC Day 1 underway (April 6-8). (Sources: Business Standard, Economic Times, RBI)

OMCs still constrained: Indian crude basket at $124/bbl (46% above RBI's $85 February assumption). No fuel price freeze has been formally announced but political pressure to do so is rising as Brent/WTI trade ~$111-116. NOP cap deadline April 10 (T-3) is creating bank-side dollar-unwinding pressure separately. RBI MPC Day 2 deliberations underway; decision Wednesday April 8. Three downside catalysts in 72 hours: deadline tonight, RBI Wed, NOP cap Friday.

MARGIN CRISIS RESOLVES — IOC/BPCL/HPCL THE LARGEST INDIAN BENEFICIARIES OF CEASEFIRE [REPORTED]. Pre-ceasefire: Indian crude basket at $120.84/bbl (46% above the RBI's $85 baseline assumption), OMCs in margin crisis, government had slashed petrol duty from Rs 13 to Rs 3 and eliminated diesel duty (March 27) leaving zero further policy space. Post-ceasefire: with Brent at $94.47 (-13.5% 1D), the Indian crude basket should fall ~$10-12/bbl over the next week, dramatically restoring OMC margins. The mechanism: (a) crude purchases at lower prices flow through to refining margins in 2-4 weeks, (b) retail fuel prices stay frozen (politically locked), so the entire price decline is captured by the OMCs, (c) the government regains policy space to reinstate fuel duties if needed (though unlikely given the political sensitivity). IOC/BPCL/HPCL margins should expand by Rs 3-5 per litre over the next month if Brent stabilizes around $95. **For the investor, IOC/BPCL/HPCL are the largest individual-stock beneficiaries of today's news in the Indian market** — bigger than the Bank Nifty rally, bigger than the rupee strengthening — because the entire crude price decline flows directly to their bottom line under frozen retail. Bank Nifty +5% reflects sector rotation; OMCs should benefit from EARNINGS revision. The brief should have flagged this — the brief led with Bank Nifty +5% but the OMC story is bigger in absolute earnings terms. Watch IOC/BPCL/HPCL pre-open prints Wednesday.

Day 45, Ceasefire Day 5. Brent at $95.20 Friday close, but Islamabad talks failed Saturday — Monday gap to $98-105 expected. Indian OMCs (IOC, BPCL, HPCL) face renewed margin pressure if oil returns above $100. March CPI releasing Monday (consensus 3.4%) — first domestic print reflecting $100+ oil. RBI oil sensitivity: $10/bbl increase = 55-60bps CPI increase. The 'ceasefire resolves oil' thesis that powered OMC recovery is under acute strain. (Source: yfinance, markets.md)

Private vs state OMC pricing divergence confirmed: Shell hiked diesel Rs 25/litre; Nayara (Rosneft-backed) hiked petrol Rs 5, diesel Rs 3 — both passing through higher crude costs to consumers (source: CNBC TV18, Sunday Guardian April 14). State OMCs (IOC, BPCL, HPCL) absorbing Rs 8-10/litre in under-recoveries, holding retail fuel prices steady despite $100+ oil. This is politically motivated — no government can hike fuel prices during a military crisis with rising inflation — but creates fiscal pressure that eventually breaks. March CPI printed 3.40% with imported inflation at 6.49% (43% of overall CPI), confirming the crisis import cost channel is dominant. Every Rs 10 fuel price hike adds 20-30 bps to headline CPI. Oil fell back to $97.98 on Trump's 'Iran deal' claim — temporary margin relief if sustained below $100, but physical-futures gap ($120 vs $98) means actual procurement costs for OMCs remain elevated.

Day 48. IRANIAN CRUDE LIFELINE ESTABLISHED BUT IMMEDIATELY THREATENED [CONFIRMED]. India received Iranian crude for the FIRST TIME since 2019 — tankers Felicity at Sikka and Jaya at Paradip, approximately 4 million barrels total (sources: BusinessToday, Bloomberg, Kashmir Observer). Reliance Industries was seeking government approval for four more Iranian vessels — a strategy that the approaching Iran sanctions waiver expiry (April 19, 4 days away) now directly threatens. The supply picture for OMCs is squeezed from three directions: (1) Russian oil waiver expired April 11 — India's largest alternative source now carries secondary sanctions risk, (2) Iran waiver expires April 19 — threatening the newly established Iranian supply, (3) physical Hormuz blockade disrupts what remains. India imports ~5.5M bpd (~85% of consumption) with strategic reserves at ~160M barrels (30 days buffer). Brent futures at $95.21 provide headline margin relief, but physical Dated Brent at $120-144 means actual procurement costs for OMCs remain far above the futures price. Indian markets rallied INTO this squeeze: Nifty +1.5% to 24,194, crossing 200-DMA — increasing asymmetry if waiver expires without extension.

Day 52 / WAIVER T-0 (expires Sunday midnight UTC / Monday 05:30 IST). Bessent publicly confirmed: 'We will not be renewing the general license on Russian oil, and we will not be renewing the general license on Iranian oil.' Business Today April 18 nuance: separate 30-day Russian waiver extension for India carved out — so 'no renewals' headline specifically forecloses Iranian continuity while leaving Russian supply protected. Operational totals as of Saturday night IST: IOC Jaya 2M bbl discharged (first Indian Iranian-crude import in 7 years); Reliance Felicity 2M bbl discharged at Sikka; Reliance rejected Derya + Lenore Saturday on compliance grounds. India MEA summoned Iran's Ambassador Mohammad Fathali Saturday evening; Foreign Secretary Misri conveyed 'deep concern' over Sanmar Herald (bridge-window damage, ~2M bbl Iraqi crude) and Jag Arnav (Indian-flagged, ~2M bbl, forced to retreat westward). First formal India-Iran démarche of 52-day crisis — narrow framing (concern not condemn) preserves off-ramp but ratchet unprecedented. IOC + BPCL + HPCL have not reversed. Post-expiry secondary sanctions risk on anyone lifting Iranian crude absent new waiver. At Monday Brent $95+ the OMC daily USD demand $250-300M becomes binding on spot rupee — transmission to USD/INR expected to re-test Rs93+ Monday. Supply recalibration to Iraq/UAE/Saudi/Africa/US/Latin America at higher delivered cost is Q2-Q3 structural theme. (Sources: Bessent via multiple US outlets + Business Today, Business Standard, Reuters India, Bloomberg India bureau, intel.md A3/A7)

BESSENT IRANIAN-OIL WAIVER ELAPSED 05:30 IST MONDAY — STRUCTURAL ZERO MARGINAL IRANIAN SUPPLY; RUSSIAN WAIVER RENEWED (30-DAY) PRESERVES ~1.9 MBPD [CONFIRMED]. Incremental Iranian supply to Indian OMCs structurally zero from 05:30 IST Monday onward — Bessent's non-renewal executed without extension, carve-out, or wind-down. Russian-oil waiver RENEWED (30-day India-specific extension) despite Bessent's April 15 "neither" statement — preserves March baseline ~1.9 mbpd Russian flow to Indian refiners. Reliance pre-positioned earlier by rejecting Derya and Lenore. IOC/BPCL/HPCL Monday -1.5 to -2.5% on Brent gap. Reliance Q4 earnings this week — crisis-beneficiary through refining margins, oil-sourcing complexity penalized.

STRUCTURAL FUEL-PRICING DETERIORATION CONFIRMED — ₹20/L PETROL + ₹100/L DIESEL LOSSES; PUMP PRICES FROZEN ~4 YEARS [CONFIRMED]. Per Business Standard April 23 + government statements: state-owned fuel retailers (IOC, BPCL, HPCL) incurring losses of ~₹20/litre on petrol and ~₹100/litre on diesel as pump prices remain frozen for ~4 years despite surge in global oil prices. India crude basket stood at $100.41 on Friday April 24 vs February average $69.01. The ₹100/litre diesel loss is the sharpest reading on this — implies OMC subsidy burden is now genuinely structural, not transitory. Three policy options, all costly: (a) pass-through pricing (politically costly with state elections), (b) OMC equity injection (fiscally costly), (c) Iran-rupee oil purchase via Chabahar (geopolitically costly with US). Petroleum Ministry confirmed losses but said 'no plan to hike price.' The fuel-pricing constraint is a hard arithmetic floor on India's diplomatic flexibility — the longer Brent stays elevated, the more India's MEA will be pushed toward the Iran-rupee oil track regardless of US preferences. Branch 1 outcome compounds the arithmetic: every additional week of $105+ Brent adds ~₹2,800 crore to OMC subsidy burden. (Sources: Business Standard, Government Petroleum Ministry, PPAC)

RBI ASKED OMCs TO USE CRISIS-ERA SPECIAL CREDIT FACILITIES — IOC/HPCL/BPCL DOLLAR ROUTING NOW RBI-ADMINISTERED [CONFIRMED Reuters via MarketScreener India + ORF Sunday-Monday]. RBI instructed state-run oil refiners (IOC, HPCL, BPCL — controlling ~50% of India's 5.2M bpd refining capacity) to curb spot dollar buying and use special credit facilities for their dollar needs. Described as 'crisis-era measures' that have been 'in place for about two weeks' (activated ~April 13-15). This formalises into operational policy what yesterday's brief framed as a 'hard arithmetic floor' on India's diplomatic flexibility. Mechanism: OMCs were sourcing ~$250-300M/day in spot USD demand to fund crude imports at $100+ Brent — that flow now routed through RBI-administered credit windows rather than the open spot market. Second confirmed RBI crisis-era measure of war (after cumulative ~$5B+ FX reserve drawdowns). Branch 3 Brent sub-$103 sustained 2-3 sessions = facility wind-down likely. The OMC subsidy burden remains structurally unchanged — ₹100/L diesel + ₹20/L petrol losses — but the dollar-side pressure on the rupee is administratively contained. (Sources: Reuters via MarketScreener India, ORF, Business Standard)

BRANCH 3 CONFIRMED MONDAY — IOC/BPCL/HPCL EXPECTED +1.5-3% ON DIESEL MARGIN RECOVERY [REPORTED]. Brent gapped -4.6% to $100.47 Sunday Globex / Monday Asia tape. Mechanism: lower crude → diesel margin compression unwinds → ₹100/L diesel loss arithmetic improves at the marginal barrel → IOC/BPCL/HPCL Monday cash open expected +1.5-3%. Petroleum Planning & Analysis Cell (PPAC) publishing daily international crude rates — Economic Times read this as a tell that GoI is preparing public for fuel-price pass-through. Goodreturns/V3Cars Monday fuel prices: petrol ₹94.77/L Delhi, diesel ₹87.67/L Delhi, petrol ₹103.54/L Mumbai. Government forced choice within 30 days driven by ₹100/L diesel + ₹20/L petrol losses + RBI special-credit-facility constraint: (a) raise pump prices (politically costly given upcoming state elections); (b) absorb OMC losses via fiscal subsidy (~₹2,800 crore/week); (c) restart Iran-rupee oil track via Chabahar despite US blockade pressure; (d) wait for ceasefire/Brent normalisation. Branch 3 Brent sub-$103 sustained reduces urgency on (a)-(c). Union Bank of India: 'every $10/bbl move in oil price significantly widens the current account deficit and pushes inflation higher.' (Sources: Sunday Globex Brent tape, Goodreturns, V3Cars, Economic Times, PPAC, Union Bank of India)
signals.verification: CONFIRMED Sunday Globex repricing + retail fuel price data.

edges.context: Day 60 Monday. Brent gapped -4.6% to $100.47 Sunday Globex = Branch 3 confirmed. Diesel margin compression unwinds at lower crude. ₹100/L diesel + ₹20/L petrol structural losses persist but marginal barrel arithmetic improves. IOC/BPCL/HPCL expected +1.5-3% Monday cash open on diesel margin recovery.

Day 60 Monday. Government forced choice within 30 days (by 2026-05-27): pass-through, fiscal subsidy, Iran-rupee track via Chabahar, or wait for Brent normalisation. PPAC publishing daily international crude rates = preparation tell for pass-through. ₹100/L diesel + ₹20/L petrol losses; pump prices frozen ~4 years.

Day 60 Monday. Branch 3 confirmed: IOC/BPCL/HPCL expected +1.5-3% on diesel margin recovery at cash open. Sector contribution to Nifty rebound. RBI special-credit-facility removes acute funding stress on OMC balance sheets.

Day 60 Monday. RBI special-credit-facility for OMCs CONFIRMED active ~2 weeks. Routes ~$250-300M/day OMC USD demand through RBI-administered credit windows rather than spot market. Second confirmed RBI crisis-era measure of war. Branch 3 sub-$103 sustained = facility wind-down likely.

trigger_points.condition: Government instructs OMCs to freeze fuel prices despite $110+ oil
trigger_points.mechanism: Would signal government prioritizing political stability over fiscal health. OMC stocks would crash. Fiscal deficit widens. Rating agency scrutiny increases.

trigger_points.condition: OMC fuel-price pass-through announced OR Iran-rupee oil track via Chabahar restart within 30 days (by 2026-05-27)
trigger_points.mechanism: Government forced choice driven by ₹100/L diesel + ₹20/L petrol losses + RBI special-credit-facility constraint. PPAC daily international crude rate publication = preparation tell for pass-through. Pass-through = +20-30bps headline CPI per ₹10 hike, OMC margin restoration, politically explosive pre-state-elections. Chabahar = US sanctions confrontation, INR-positive (rupee oil track), bypasses dollar rationing. Either resolution unwinds the OMC structural compression.
